channel.title: SEAtoday - Food & Drink
channel.description: Upcoming events for SEAtoday - Food & Drink

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Eric-Paul Riege at Burke Artist Studio
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

To celebrate the opening weekend of Eric-Paul Riege: ojo|-|ólǫ́, visit the artist offsite at the Burke Artist Studio, located in the Northwest Native Art Gallery at the Burke Museum, just a few blocks from the Henry. As part of Free First Thursday at the Burke, visitors will have the chance to watch the artist at work and speak with Riege about his process. ojo|-|ólǫ́ is based on Eric-Paul Riege's material research and engagement with the Navajo collections held by the Burke Museum, as well as the Brown University's Haffenreffer Museum of Anthropology. The resulting new body of work is informed by ancestral knowledge and traditions contained within Indigenous objects, and which aims, in part, to redress practices of cultural dispossession by museums and other institutions. Bio Eric-Paul Riege \(Diné\) is a weaver and fiber artist working in collage, durational performance, installation, woven sculpture, and wearable art. Using weaving as both means and metaphor to tell hybrid tales that interlace stories from Diné spirituality with his own interpretations and cosmology, he understands his artworks as animate and mobile. His practice pays homage and links him to generations of weavers in his family who aid him in generating spaces of sanctuary. Riege's recent solo exhibitions include iiZiiT \[3\]: RIEGE Jewelry \+ Supply at Canal Projects, New York \(2025\), Hammer Projects: Eric Paul Riege at the Hammer Museum, Los Angeles \(2022-2023\), and Hól ́ǫ-it xistz at the Institute of Contemporary Art Miami \(2019\). His recent group exhibitions include the 24th Biennale of Sydney in Australia \(2024\), Indian Theater: Native Performance, Art, and Self-Determination \(2023\), Prospect.5 Triennial in New Orleans \(2022\), and the Toronto Biennial of Art \(2022\). He holds a BFA in Art Studio and Ecology from the University of New Mexico in Albuquerque. His work is collected by Forge Project and ICA Miami, among others. He is represented by Bockley Gallery \(MN\) and STARS Gallery \(CA\). Riege is a member of the Charcoal Streaked Division of the Red Running Into the Water clan. He was born and is based in Na'nízhoozhí \[Gallup, New Mexico\].

Event details

item.title: Membership Luncheon: Legislative Wrap
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Green River College - Kent Campus, 417 Ramsay Way, Suite 112, Room 282/283, Kent, WA

Description This luncheon focuses on discussing Kent's major development projects, growth corridors, and business attraction strategies. Attendees will connect with peers, engage with speakers, and gain insights for business growth

Event details

item.title: FREE OFFICE AND COFFEE FRIDAYS!
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Spaces- Stadium, 450 Alaskan Way South #Suite 200, Seattle, WA

Join us for the day and treat yourself to one of our cozy, furnished office spaces. The coffee's on us!

Event details

item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

item.title: Celebrate Easter at Lotte Hotel Seattle
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Charlotte Restaurant & Lounge, Lotte Hotel Seattle, 809 5th Ave 16th Floor,, Seattle, WA

Celebrate the holiday with a refined prix-fixe brunch at **Lotte Hotel Seattle’s**, **Charlotte Restaurant & Lounge**, featuring elevated dishes like Dungeness crab tartlet, ube pancakes and lamb benedict, accompanied by live jazz from Second Dimension. Guests can enjoy stunning views of Elliott Bay in a luxurious, family-friendly setting. Reservations are recommended.

Event details

item.title: Seattle Sing-Along Brunch
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Admiral Pub, 2306 California Avenue Southwest, Seattle, WA

Brunch, Mimosas and Sing-along to the greatest pop hits of all time. Tickets include Brunch Buffet & party swag like inflatable Microphones!

Event details

item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Willows Lodge, 14580 Northeast 145th Street, Woodinville, WA

Get ready to hop into spring with an egg-citing Easter Brunch Buffet at Willows Lodge - a feast you won't want to miss!

Event details

item.title: Kiana Easter Brunch & Egg Hunt
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Kiana Lodge, 14976 Sandy Hook Rd NE, Poulsbo, WA

Kiana Lodge Easter Buffet & Egg Hunt Sun April 5th, 2026 Brunch Buffet 11:00AM - 02:00PM,Easter Bunny & Egg Hunt 12:00PM Come visit with the Easter bunny and enjoy a tasty Easter brunch at Kiana Lodge. Kids are encouraged to bring their own basket so they can find the hidden eggs filled with goodies! Adults: $54.60, Children 5-12: $21.84, 5 and Under: $5.46 Beverages: Cash Bar with Mimosa & Bloody Mary Specials Highchairs available in limited quantity please email info@kianalodge.com to reserve. All Children must be accompanied by an adult, please provide your own basket for the egg hunt. Menu: Fresh Sliced Seasonal Fruit Assorted Breakfast Pastries Scrambled Eggs Crisp Bacon Savory Sausage Links Sliced Glazed Ham Cedar Ridge Potatoes Spam Fried Rice Croissant French Toast Mixed Green Salad with balsamic dressing Kiana Signature Salmon Chef Carved Prime Rib with Creamy Horseradish & Au Jus Teriyaki Chicken Steamed Rice Seasonal Roasted Vegetables Omelet Station Dessert Buffet

Event details

item.title: Raised By TV Events Presents: *ALL AGES* The Office Trivia Night
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Clock-Out Lounge, 4864 Beacon Ave S, Seattle, WA

RAISED By TV brings you:THE OFFICE Trivia Night!50 BRAND NEW questions about your favorite Scranton palsMon April 6th$7 ONLINE ADVANCE: \{plus ticketing fees\}$9 Day of & AT THE DOOR \{plus ticketing fees\}Doors open at 5:30. Trivia starts at 6:30!all ages til 8pm!Tables are first come first serve. \*\*PRIZES\*\*The RAISED BY TV gang has put together some killer prize packages. Teams of 1-5 are welcome. Prizes for 1st, 2nd, t have to be good at trivia just come on down to relive some Scranton shenanigans with us. \*\*FOOD & DRINKS\*\*The Clock-Out has bar and full Stevies Famous Pizza food service; one of the things that makes it our favorite place.The show is available on PeacockWe hope you can join RbyTV for a safe night of fun and brains.Brandon Ryan

Event details

item.title: 5-Course Gil Family Estates Winemaker Dinner at 1 Hotel Seattle
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 1 Hotel Seattle, 2125 Terry Avenue, Seattle, WA

Join the 1 Hotel Seattle team at La Loba for a special evening celebrating 110 years of Gil Family Estates with a five-course tasting menu and curated wine pairings. Guests will enjoy wines from across the Gil family portfolio—including selections from Bodegas El Nido, Shaya, and Juan Gil—presented by visiting winemakers and members of the Gil  Each course will be introduced by Chef Oscar and his culinary team, highlighting seasonal flavors designed to complement the wines. The evening begins with a welcome toast and continues through five paired courses in our Emerald Private Dining Room. Seating is communal to encourage conversation and connection among fellow wine lovers.

Event details

item.title: Silent Book Club Night!!
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Quinn's Pub, 1001 East Pike Street, Seattle, WA

Welcome to Capitol Hill's Silent Book Club! We meet twice a month at a different restaurant or coffee shop around Capitol Hill to show support to our wonderful community! We aren't your typical book club. There is no assigned reading and you're welcome to use any medium to enjoy your story \(i.e. paperback, audiobook, kindle, etc.\). This is a judgement free zone. If you're into fiction, non-fiction, or graphic novels, bring whatever you enjoy! Our mission is to create a welcoming environment for a diverse group to share their love of books while supporting local businesses in the process. Whether you're a bookworm or just looking for a relaxing night out, this event is perfect for anyone who wants to be a part of our bookish community. How it works: 6:30 PM - Get settled, order food/drinks, meet fellow book lovers 7:00 PM - Read whatever book you like in your preferred platform 8:00 PM - Socialize, keep reading, or pay your bill and enjoy the rest of your night 9:00 PM - Quinn's Pub closes Come alone or bring a friend. Don't miss out on this unique experience! Follow us on Instagram to stay up to date on our upcoming events: @silentbookclubcapitolhill. \*\*\*This is a free event but to show our appreciation to Quinn's, a purchase is required.

Event details

item.title: My Dinner with SAM
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Celebrate the art of food at intimate chef dinners inspired by SAM's three unique sites. Dine in artful and inspiring settings while enjoying a dynamic evening with culinary artists, local producers and makers, and more. Stay tuned, tickets to these limited-seating dinners will be available in January. Dinner dates and venues Thursday, March 12 6:30 pm Guest chef: Preeti Agarwal, Meesha and Kricket Club Seattle Asian Art Museum Wednesday, April 8 6:30 pm Guest chef: Kevin Smith, Beast & Cleaver, Little Beast, and The Peasant Seattle Art Museum May Olympic Sculpture Park Guest chef & date to come My Dinner with SAM is a major fundraising event and all proceeds benefit SAM's education, community engagement, and exhibition programs. 3-Dinner Series VIP Tickets $1,250 per person One VIP ticket to each dinner Recognition as a series sponsor on all evening programs and dedicated event emails Special take-away at each dinner Complimentary parking at Seattle Art Museum and Olympic Sculpture Park Individual Dinner Tickets Benefactor - $550 per person One ticket to one dinner Recognition on evening program Special take-away at the dinner Complimentary parking at Seattle Art Museum and Olympic Sculpture Park Patron - $350 per person One ticket to one dinner All but $150 per dinner is tax-deductible. If you are unable to attend the event, please consider making a donation to support My Dinner with SAM. To make a tax-deductible donation, please contact us at dinnerwithSAM@seattleartmuseum.org or 206.654.3123.

Event details

item.title: April Luncheon
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Youngstown Cultural Arts Center, 4408 Delridge Wy SW, Seattle, WA

Description Explore new horizons and connect with fellow professionals at the West Seattle Chamber&'s monthly luncheons, where engaging discussions and networking opportunities lay the groundwork for you to grow your business.

item.title: Best of CID Food Tour
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Wing Luke Museum, 719 S. King Street, Seattle, WA

Best of CID Food Tour Friday, April 10 | 4:00PM-6:30PM General admission to the Museum is included with your tour ticket.

Event details

item.title: Live Music Happy Hour Joe Casalini Trio
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: The Royal Room, 5000 Rainier Ave South, Seattle, WA

Join us for Live Music Happy Hour with the Joe Casalini Trio. The Casalini Trio has played happy hours at the Royal Room since 2019. They play laid-back, swinging versions of great tunes by Duke Ellington, Billy Strayhorn, Hoagie Carmichael, Phillip Johnston, and other great American songwriters of the last 100 years. The happy hour usually lasts about an hour and a half. Good drinks, good people, good ambience, and there’s never a cover charge, although the band often encourages donations to local food banks and other worthy causes.

Event details

item.title: April Open Mic Night
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Wm. Grassie Duvall Tasting Room, 15705 Main St NE, Duvall, WA

Hosted by Jeff Crookall & Kim Dietz, it's open mic night! Get in early, grab a glass of wine or beer and add your name to the sign-up list! Show us what ya got or just sit back and enjoy the tunes! This is an inclusive and supportive group of local musicians!

Event details

item.title: Hill Talk: Stoup Brewing Capitol Hill
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Stoup Brewing, 1158 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Description HillTalk at Stoup Brewing \(Capitol Hill\) Tuesday, April 14  4:00-6:00 PM Join GSBA for our next HillTalk as we gather at Stoup Brewing's Capitol Hill taproom for a candid conversation about what's happening in our neighborhood and what it means for local businesses. HillTalk brings together GSBA members, business owners, community partners, and neighborhood leaders for real dialogue and practical updates. Whether you're navigating public safety concerns, city policy changes, foot traffic shifts, or new opportunities for collaboration, this is your space to stay informed and connected. Location: Stoup Brewing - Capitol Hill What is HillTalk? HillTalk is a casual, community-centered gathering where Capitol Hill businesses and community members can hear timely updates, ask questions, share perspective, and connect with the people shaping our neighborhood. What to Expect Neighborhood updates impacting Capitol Hill businesses Connection with fellow business owners and community partners A welcoming, relaxed setting to build relationships and stay engaged Come As You Are Grab a pint, pull up a chair, and be part of the conversation shaping Capitol Hill's future.

Event details

item.title: Geeks Who Drink Trivia
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Chuck's Hop Shop Greenwood, 656 NW 85th St, Seattle, WA

Q: Who helped pioneer the craft beer movement in Seattle by creating a friendly inclusive environment along side one of the best beer selections in the city? A: Chuck's Hop Shop Greenwood! Join us for Trivia Night every Tuesday. Flex your brain while enjoying beers and BBQ from Woodshop BBQ! Free to play with prizes for the top three teams!

Event details
